name: Santander Bank Polska S.A.
summary: Santander Bank Polska S.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides various banking products and services for individuals, small or medium-sized enterprises, and corporate clients. It offers saving, escrow, foreign currency, company, agribusiness, and systematic accounts; cash, home, mortgage, investment, revolving, business vehicle, and technology loans, as well as agro investment, construction, bridging, production, disaster, and land loans; debit, credit, and business cards; and payment terminals, overdraft facility, and electronic and internet banking services. The company also provides term, structured, short term, and currency deposits; insurance products; and personal finances, investment advisory services, investment funds, brokerage, and global banking services. In addition, it offers foreign trade services, including bank guarantees, payments, documentary and financial collection, and foreign and own letter of credit; forfaiting and currency exchange; and leasing of machines and devices, passenger cars, auto, and truck. Further, the company provides transaction support and liquidity management services; arable land, ongoing, and investment financing; export, import, foreign trade guarantees, working capital financing, and debt capital and capital market services; and correspondence banking, trade financing, and fiduciary services, as well as global transaction services for bank. It operates through a network 665 outlets, as well as 1700 ATMs and cash deposit machines in Poland. The company was formerly known as Bank Zachodni WBK S.A. and changed its name to Santander Bank Polska S.A. in September 2018. The company was founded in 1857 and is headquartered in Warsaw, Poland. Santander Bank Polska S.A. is a subsidiary of Banco Santander S.A.
